attributedString.string:
Johannes Gutenberg’s
16 / Work on the printing press began when he partnered with Andreas Heilmann, owner of a paper mill. Having previously worked as a goldsmith, Gutenberg made skillful use of the knowledge of metals he had learned as a craftsman. He was the first to make type from an alloy of lead, tin, and antimony, which was critical for producing durable type that produced high-quality printed books and proved to be much better suited for printing than all other known materials.

Using the ttf “Saira-Parametric-April” I composed actual size specimens of the default, min and max opsz. XTRA was kept to the values of 25/100/125, except in opsz8, where values were increased slightly.

The min weight at 144 is diffecult to blend as the gvars need some tuning, and XTRA needs more completed glyphs to reach a suitable wdth min test result.

The 144 at the max width could use weight increased by extending XOPQ and YOPQ another 20-25% of what it is now, thought the current max is all the lowercase can embolden in YOPQ.

Overall, the completed glyphs demo that this family can have extensive support for sizes, weights and widths, which once complete can also have a contrast axis and ROND, would be extrordinary.
